evidence: both describe creating a CLI harness from source code; 'Build a complete CLI harness' (command title) co-occurs with 'Full CLI Generation' (README heading) and 'every generated CLI' in the same files — same concept of producing a CLI artifact from a codebase

both label numbered sequential stages of a pipeline; 'Phase 0-7' headings in cli-anything.md vs 'Step 1-6' headings in refine.md — same position role (h3 section header for numbered pipeline stage) across parallel command documents

both describe confirming correctness; 'validate a CLI harness against HARNESS.md standards' (validate.md) and 'output verification' / 'verify the output' (HARNESS.md, cli-anything.md) name the same quality-checking concept in overlapping files

both describe examining the software source; '### Step 2: Analyze Software Capabilities' and 'Re-scan the software source' appear in the same step of refine.md, naming the same pass over the target codebase with two different verbs

both describe growing CLI coverage in the refine context; opencode-commands frontmatter says 'expand coverage and add missing capabilities' while the same file body says 'improve coverage of the software's functions' — co-occurring synonyms for one concept

both describe the feature-set of the target software; 'software's full capabilities' and 'functionality area' appear interchangeably in refine.md and opencode-commands to label what the target software can do

mixed signals: 'The harness itself is a wrapper' (dify-workflow SKILL.md) directly equates them, but most 'wrapper' uses describe CLIs that wrap existing tools while 'harness' is the standard term for the full generated CLI package
